Side by side: price, parts, labor, warranty

This looks like a list of dealer service recommendations

Dealers commonly bundle several optional services into one visit (filters, fluid flushes, alignments, balances). The total below is the sum of every recommendation — you almost never need all of them at once. Check the upsell flags below and ask which items can wait for the next service interval.

Possible Unnecessary Services

These services on your quote may not be needed. This is informational -- discuss with your mechanic.

Pollen Filter - Replace
This service may not be needed because cabin/pollen filters typically cost $20-40 in parts; dealer rates of $150+ usually include 1+ hours of labor for what's a 5-minute swap most owners can do themselves
Typical cost: $60.00
MOUNT AND BALANCE 4 TIRES
This service may not be needed because mount and balance is typically only needed when buying new tires; without a tire purchase, this charge is hard to justify
Typical cost: $80.00
PERFORM ALL WHEEL DRIVE FRONT AND REAR ALIGNMENT
This service may not be needed because alignment is recommended after suspension work or noticeable pull/uneven wear; on a maintenance visit without those triggers it's often an upsell
Typical cost: $130.00
TORN P/S OUTER CV BOOT
This service may not be needed because diagnostic findings labeled 'leaking' or 'torn' often appear as full-replacement quotes; ask whether a re-seal or boot-only repair is an option before authorizing the full job
Typical cost: $1,018.00
